fixture_label: Rumble Arena prepared demo: OpenAI Agents SDK vs LangGraph
coverage_notice: Prepared from narrow static inspection of pinned public repository source and documentation. No upstream code was executed. This is not runtime verification, a comprehensive project analysis, a security audit, or a universal ranking. A no_evidence_found cell describes this inspection only and must not be treated as proof that a capability is absent.

matchups.display_label: Support Agent Showdown: OpenAI Agents SDK vs LangGraph

assessment_context.title: Internal support agent proof of concept
assessment_context.use_case: Build a Python support agent with approval and tracing.

comparison_rows.label: Approval Gate Smackdown
comparison_rows.requirement: Pause a sensitive support tool call until a human approves or rejects it.

entrant_a.value: Function tools have a per-call approval gate that interrupts before execution and resumes through RunState.

entrant_b.value: A generic interrupt and Command flow can host approval input, with the gate assembled in graph logic and backed by a checkpointer.

comparison_rows.label: Restart Comeback Combo
comparison_rows.requirement: Persist a paused approval in PostgreSQL and resume it after the support service restarts.

entrant_a.value: RunState serializes approval state for later resume; the application chooses and wires the PostgreSQL storage layer.

entrant_b.value: Interrupts use persisted graph state, and the repository includes a PostgresSaver that writes checkpoints to PostgreSQL.

comparison_rows.label: Audit Bell-Ringer
comparison_rows.requirement: Emit explicit approval pause and resume callbacks to an in-process audit adapter without a hosted control plane.

entrant_b.value: GraphCallbackHandler exposes interrupt and resume hooks with checkpoint-specific lifecycle event payloads.

claims.statement: FunctionTool exposes an always-on or per-call needs_approval gate that interrupts before tool execution and resolves through RunState.
claims.why_it_matters: A support action such as a refund or cancellation can be held before its side effect occurs.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: needs_approval interrupts the run before execution; RunState approves or rejects the specific call.

claims.statement: LangGraph interrupt pauses graph execution, surfaces a value to the client, and resumes through Command using persisted graph state.
claims.why_it_matters: A support workflow can place a human decision point before a sensitive node, although the application defines that approval policy in its graph.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: interrupt pauses execution, surfaces a value to the client, resumes through Command, and requires a checkpointer.

claims.statement: RunState provides JSON-compatible serialization and deserialization intended to resume a paused run later.
claims.why_it_matters: A support service can store pending approval work in its chosen database or queue and reconstruct it after a restart.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: to_json serializes run state to a JSON-compatible dictionary that can resume the run later.
from_json reconstructs RunState from a dictionary created by to_json.
RunState can store pending work in an application database or queue and recreate it later.

claims.statement: LangGraph provides a PostgresSaver implementation that persists checkpoints used by durable, resumable graph workflows.
claims.why_it_matters: The support service can use a repository-provided PostgreSQL checkpointer for approval state instead of designing the persistence adapter from scratch.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: PostgresSaver is a checkpointer that stores checkpoints in a Postgres database.
PostgresSaver.put saves a checkpoint and its associated configuration to the Postgres database.
A client resumes with Command; interrupt requires a checkpointer because it relies on persisted graph state.

claims.statement: GraphCallbackHandler exposes graph-specific on_interrupt and on_resume callbacks with checkpoint-linked event payloads.
claims.why_it_matters: The support service can feed approval lifecycle events into an in-process audit adapter without making a hosted control plane part of the callback path.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: Interrupt and resume events carry checkpoint identifiers and namespaces for paused and resumed graph execution.
GraphCallbackHandler defines on_interrupt and on_resume hooks and can be passed through graph invocation callbacks.
